From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

3rd edition of the Pan American Games

III Pan American Games

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Poster of the 1959 Pan American Games | |
| Host | [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), United States |
| Nations | 25 |
| Athletes | 2,263 |
| Events | 166 in 18 [sports](/wiki/Pan_American_Games_sports "Pan American Games sports") |
| Opening | August 27 |
| Closing | September 7 |
| Opened by | [Milton S. Eisenhower](/wiki/Milton_S._Eisenhower "Milton S. Eisenhower")[[1]](#cite_note-eisenhower-1) |
| Main venue | [Soldier Field](/wiki/Soldier_Field "Soldier Field") |
| ← [1955 Mexico City](/wiki/1955_Pan_American_Games "1955 Pan American Games")  [1963 São Paulo](/wiki/1963_Pan_American_Games "1963 Pan American Games") → | |

The **1959 Pan American Games**, officially known as the **III Pan American Games** and commonly known as **Chicago 1959**, were held in [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"), [Illinois](/wiki/Illinois "Illinois"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") between August 28 and September 7, 1959.

## Host city selection

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Host city selection")]

Main article: [Bids for the 1959 Pan American Games](/wiki/Bids_for_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Bids for the 1959 Pan American Games")

One city initially submitted a bid to host the 1959 Pan American Games that was recognized by the [Pan American Sports Organization](/wiki/Pan_American_Sports_Organization "Pan American Sports Organization") (PASO), along with three cities that had submitted bids, then later withdrew their bids. On March 11, 1955, at the IV Pan American Congress in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), PASO selected [Cleveland, Ohio](/wiki/Cleveland,_Ohio "Cleveland, Ohio") unanimously to host the III Pan American Games.[[2]](#cite_note-LSU-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-ET-4)

On April 15, 1957, Cleveland asked PASO to be relieved of its assignment as the host city as the city believed it was not properly prepared to host the Games. Subsequently, both [Guatemala City](/wiki/Guatemala_City "Guatemala City") and [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") informed PASO that they would not be able to host the Games either. Two cities came forward as candidates to host the games, [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago") and [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"). On August 3, 1957, Chicago was selected to host the Games over São Paulo by a vote of 13 to 6.[[2]](#cite_note-LSU-2)[[5]](#cite_note-Tribune-5)

## Organization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Organization")]

Once Chicago took over as host city following Cleveland's withdrawal, there were 18 months left to organize the games. The games were held on-schedule nonetheless. The games were the first Pan American Games to be held during the [Northern Hemisphere](/wiki/Northern_Hemisphere "Northern Hemisphere")'s [summer](/wiki/Summer "Summer"). The previous two editions were held in March.[[5]](#cite_note-Tribune-5)

In the months following the awarding of the Games to Chicago, PASO leadership became seriously concerned that Chicago's preparations for the event were insufficient. Particularly, they felt that the event's director, Colonel Jack Reilly, was treating the Games as an ordinary Chicago event rather than an international competition on par with the Olympics. In the summer of 1958, then-current [IOC president](/wiki/President_of_the_International_Olympic_Committee "President of the International Olympic Committee") and PASO honorary president [Avery Brundage](/wiki/Avery_Brundage "Avery Brundage"), himself an American, privately said that the Chicago organizers "haven't the faintest idea of the magnitude of the task they have assumed" and predicted that the Games would be "the most dismal fiasco in the history of international sport". Publicly, he warned that "unless greater effort is put forth to prepare for the 1959 Pan American Games, both the city and the nation may be disgraced in the eyes of the world". PASO considered cancelling the Games entirely before Chicago's organizing committee was reorganized and Reilly was replaced.[[6]](#cite_note-macaloon-6)

## The Games

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: The Games")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e8/1959PanAmericanGamesAlternateLogo.jpg/250px-1959PanAmericanGamesAlternateLogo.jpg)](/wiki/File:1959PanAmericanGamesAlternateLogo.jpg)

Alternative poster in Spanish

The Games opened on August 27, 1959, in sunny 90 °F (32 °C) heat before 40,000 people at [Soldier Field](/wiki/Soldier_Field "Soldier Field"). The opening ceremony was preceded by [a monthlong torch relay](/wiki/1959_Pan_American_Games_torch_relay "1959 Pan American Games torch relay") from Mexico City to Chicago. The torch itself was symbolic: instead of a burning flame, the "Atomic Torch" consisted of a blue lightbulb surrounded by a [chromium](/wiki/Chromium "Chromium")-plated model of an atom. The torch was designed by staff at [Argonne National Laboratory](/wiki/Argonne_National_Laboratory "Argonne National Laboratory") near Chicago.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) President [Dwight D. Eisenhower](/wiki/Dwight_D._Eisenhower "Dwight D. Eisenhower") was expected to attend the opening ceremony, but he canceled his appearance in favor of a diplomatic trip to Europe. Eisenhower's brother [Milton](/wiki/Milton_S._Eisenhower "Milton S. Eisenhower") attended the ceremony in his place.[[1]](#cite_note-eisenhower-1)[[9]](#cite_note-hersh-9)

The event was marred by numerous organizational failures and poor-quality facilities. For some sports, such as shooting, no practice facilities were provided at all.[[10]](#cite_note-10) Manuel Gonzalez Guerra, who would become president of the [Cuban Olympic Committee](/wiki/Cuban_Olympic_Committee "Cuban Olympic Committee") during the [Castro era](/wiki/Political_career_of_Fidel_Castro "Political career of Fidel Castro"), referred to the 1959 Games as "the worst ever,"[[9]](#cite_note-hersh-9) and his comments were in line with the negative perceptions of many other Latin Americans who traveled to Chicago for the Games.[[6]](#cite_note-macaloon-6)

Male athletes were housed in dormitories at the [University of Chicago](/wiki/University_of_Chicago "University of Chicago"), the [Ferry Hall School](/wiki/Ferry_Hall_School "Ferry Hall School"), and [North Central College](/wiki/North_Central_College "North Central College"), while female athletes stayed at the [Shoreland Hotel](/wiki/Shoreland_Hotel "Shoreland Hotel").[[9]](#cite_note-hersh-9) On September 7, hours before the closing ceremony, Brazilian rower [Ronaldo Duncan Arantes](/w/index.php?title=Ronaldo_Duncan_Arantes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ronaldo Duncan Arantes (page does not exist)") was found dead with bullet wounds at North Central College. Arantes' brother [Rômulo](/wiki/R%C3%B4mulo_Arantes "Rômulo Arantes"), a fellow athlete at the Games, reported that Ronaldo had brought money to Chicago with him to purchase firearms.[[11]](#cite_note-11) No one was ever charged in Arantes' death.[[6]](#cite_note-macaloon-6)

## Sports and venues

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sports and venues")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Abanderado_en_los_juegos_panamericanos_1959_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Abanderado_en_los_juegos_panamericanos_1959_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Abanderado_en_los_juegos_panamericanos_1959_(cropped).jpg)

Opening ceremonies at [Soldier Field](/wiki/Soldier_Field "Soldier Field"). Wrestler [Mario Tovar González](/wiki/Mario_Tovar_Gonz%C3%A1lez "Mario Tovar González") can be seen serving as [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico")'s flag bearer.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Chicago_-_Navy_Pier_%284284002308%29_%28Chicago_Fire_Department_Gymnasium_at_Navy_Pier%29.jpg/250px-Chicago_-_Navy_Pier_%284284002308%29_%28Chicago_Fire_Department_Gymnasium_at_Navy_Pier%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chicago_-_Navy_Pier_(4284002308)_(Chicago_Fire_Department_Gymnasium_at_Navy_Pier).jpg)

Gymnasium at Navy Pier, site of the Gymnastics competition

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/Alumni_Gym%2C_Loyola_University_Chicago.JPG/250px-Alumni_Gym%2C_Loyola_University_Chicago.JPG)](/wiki/File:Alumni_Gym,_Loyola_University_Chicago.JPG)

[Alumni Gym](/wiki/Alumni_Gym_(Loyola_University_Chicago) "Alumni Gym (Loyola University Chicago)"), venue for men's basketball (photographed in 2009)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/19970628_03_Wrigley_Fiield_%285864821081%29.jpg/250px-19970628_03_Wrigley_Fiield_%285864821081%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:19970628_03_Wrigley_Fiield_(5864821081).jpg)

[Wrigley Field](/wiki/Wrigley_Field "Wrigley Field"), a venue for baseball (photographed in 1997)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e2/CK_193%2C_Comiskey_Park_%28NBY_648%29.jpg/250px-CK_193%2C_Comiskey_Park_%28NBY_648%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:CK_193,_Comiskey_Park_(NBY_648).jpg)

[Comiskey Park](/wiki/Comiskey_Park "Comiskey Park"), a venue for baseball

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/Athletics_pictogram.svg/20px-Athletics_pictogram.svg.png) [Athletics](/wiki/Sport_of_athletics "Sport of athletics") ([details](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Athletics at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Soldier Field](/wiki/Soldier_Field "Soldier Field")[[12]](#cite_note-stadiumanditscity-12)
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3c/Baseball_pictogram.svg/20px-Baseball_pictogram.svg.png) [Baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") ([details](/wiki/Baseball_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Baseball at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Wrigley Field](/wiki/Wrigley_Field "Wrigley Field") and [Comiskey Park](/wiki/Comiskey_Park "Comiskey Park")[[13]](#cite_note-herald-13)
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/Basketball_pictogram.svg/20px-Basketball_pictogram.svg.png) [Basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") ([details](/wiki/Basketball_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Basketball at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Alumni Gym](/wiki/Alumni_Gym_(Loyola_University_Chicago) "Alumni Gym (Loyola University Chicago)") (men's) and [Oak Park High School](/wiki/Oak_Park_and_River_Forest_High_School "Oak Park and River Forest High School") (women's)[[14]](#cite_note-LATimes-14)[[15]](#cite_note-lyke-15)
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Boxing_pictogram.svg/20px-Boxing_pictogram.svg.png) [Boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing") ([details](/wiki/Boxing_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Boxing at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at Northwest Armory
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/86/Cycling_%28road%29_pictogram.svg/20px-Cycling_%28road%29_pictogram.svg.png) [Cycling](/wiki/Cycle_sport "Cycle sport") ([details](/wiki/Cycling_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Cycling at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Gately Stadium](/wiki/Gately_Stadium "Gately Stadium") *temporary venue* (Track Cycling)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/02/Fencing_pictogram.svg/20px-Fencing_pictogram.svg.png) [Fencing](/wiki/Fencing "Fencing") ([details](/w/index.php?title=Fencing_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fencing at the 1959 Pan American Games (page does not exist)"))
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cf/Football_pictogram.svg/20px-Football_pictogram.svg.png) [Football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") ([details](/wiki/Football_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Football at the 1959 Pan American Games")) (soccer) at [Hanson Stadium](/wiki/Hanson_Stadium "Hanson Stadium")[[17]](#cite_note-17) and [Soldier Field](/wiki/Soldier_Field "Soldier Field")[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/12/Gymnastics_%28artistic%29_pictogram.svg/20px-Gymnastics_%28artistic%29_pictogram.svg.png) [Gymnastics](/wiki/Gymnastics "Gymnastics") ([details](/wiki/Gymnastics_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Gymnastics at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Navy Pier](/wiki/Navy_Pier "Navy Pier")[[14]](#cite_note-LATimes-14)
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0e/Modern_pentathlon_pictogram_%28pre-2025%29.svg/20px-Modern_pentathlon_pictogram_%28pre-2025%29.svg.png) [Modern pentathlon](/wiki/Modern_pentathlon "Modern pentathlon") ([details](/wiki/Modern_pentathlon_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Modern pentathlon at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Waukegan](/wiki/Waukegan "Waukegan") Shooting Range (shooting), [Great Lakes Naval Training Center](/wiki/Great_Lakes_Naval_Training_Center "Great Lakes Naval Training Center") (fencing), [Independence Grove](/wiki/Lake_County_Forest_Preserves "Lake County Forest Preserves") (equestrian show-jumping and cross-country running), and [Portage Park](/wiki/Portage_Park "Portage Park") (swimming)[[13]](#cite_note-herald-13)

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/85/Tennis_pictogram.svg/20px-Tennis_pictogram.svg.png) [Tennis](/wiki/Tennis "Tennis") ([details](/w/index.php?title=Tennis_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tennis at the 1959 Pan American Games (page does not exist)")) at Lincoln Park Tennis Club[[13]](#cite_note-herald-13)

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0e/Water_polo_pictogram.svg/20px-Water_polo_pictogram.svg.png) [Water polo](/wiki/Water_polo "Water polo") ([details](/wiki/Water_polo_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Water polo at the 1959 Pan American Games")) at [Portage Park](/wiki/Portage_Park "Portage Park")[[20]](#cite_note-20)
